sections.heading: Cooperative Agents
sections.body: In a cooperative multi-agent system, agents work together toward a shared goal — for example, a fleet of warehouse robots coordinating to fulfill orders efficiently, where one agent's success also benefits the others.

sections.heading: Competitive Agents
sections.body: In a competitive multi-agent system, agents have conflicting goals — one agent's gain can be another's loss, similar to the adversarial game-playing search covered in Unit 2, but potentially with many agents rather than just two.

sections.heading: Communication
sections.body: Communication lets agents share information, intentions, or requests with each other — enabling coordination that wouldn't be possible if each agent could only act on its own private, local observations.

sections.heading: Coordination
sections.body: Coordination is the process of aligning multiple agents' actions to achieve a good collective outcome — avoiding conflicts (like two agents claiming the same resource) and combining individual capabilities into effective joint behavior.

sections.heading: Distributed AI
sections.body: Distributed AI studies how intelligence can emerge from multiple interacting agents rather than one centralized reasoner — often more robust and scalable than a single-agent approach, since the system doesn't have one single point of failure.

quiz.question: In a cooperative multi-agent system, agents:
options: Work together toward a shared goal

quiz.question: What role does communication play in multi-agent systems?
options: It lets agents share information/intentions, enabling coordination beyond local observation alone
It only applies to competitive agents
It replaces the need for actions

quiz.question: A key advantage of distributed AI over a single centralized reasoner is:
options: It has one single point of failure
Increased robustness and scalability without one single point of failure
It requires no coordination at all
